documents.content: Instagram is a photo and video sharing social networking service. It was created by Kevin Systrom and Mike Krieger and launched in October 2010. Facebook Inc. acquired Instagram for approximately $1 billion in cash and stock in April 2012. The acquisition was completed on September 6, 2012. At the time, Instagram had 30 million registered users.

Facebook was founded by Mark Zuckerberg along with his Harvard College roommates Eduardo Saverin, Andrew McCollum, Dustin Moskovitz, and Chris Hughes in February 2004. Zuckerberg was born on May 14, 1984, in White Plains, New York. He grew up in Dobbs Ferry, New York. Facebook went public in 2012 with a valuation of $104 billion.

WhatsApp was founded in 2009 by Brian Acton and Jan Koum, both former employees of Yahoo!. Facebook announced it was acquiring WhatsApp for $19 billion in February 2014. This was Facebook's largest acquisition. WhatsApp had over 450 million monthly active users at the time. Jan Koum was born in Kyiv, Ukraine in 1976 and immigrated to the US at age 16.

Oculus VR is a virtual reality technology company founded by Palmer Luckey, Brendan Iribe, and others in 2012. Facebook acquired Oculus for $2 billion in March 2014. The acquisition included $400 million in cash and 23.1 million shares of Facebook stock.

Twitter was founded in March 2006 by Jack Dorsey, Noah Glass, Biz Stone, and Evan Williams. The company is headquartered in San Francisco. Twitter went public in November 2013 with an initial share price of $26. Elon Musk acquired Twitter in October 2022 for $44 billion.

OpenAI was founded in December 2015 by Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever, Elon Musk, and others. The company is headquartered in San Francisco. In 2019, OpenAI transitioned from a non-profit to a capped-profit structure. Microsoft invested $1 billion in OpenAI in 2019, and later invested $10 billion in January 2023. GPT-4 was released in March 2023.

Albert Einstein was born on March 14, 1879, in Ulm, Germany. He published his theory of special relativity in 1905, introducing E=mc². Einstein received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921 for his explanation of the photoelectric effect, not for relativity. He became a US citizen in 1940 and worked at the Institute for Advanced Study in Princeton until his death in 1955. His brain was preserved for scientific study.

Marie Curie was born Maria Sklodowska on November 7, 1867, in Warsaw, Poland. She moved to Paris in 1891 to study at the Sorbonne. Curie discovered polonium (named after Poland) and radium in 1898. She won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1903 (shared with Pierre Curie and Henri Becquerel) and the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1911, making her the first person to win Nobel Prizes in two different sciences. She died in 1934 from aplastic anemia caused by radiation exposure.

CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing technology was developed by Jennifer Doudna and Emmanuelle Charpentier, who published their breakthrough paper in Science in June 2012. They received the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 2020 for this work. The first clinical trial using CRISPR in humans began in China in 2016. Feng Zhang of the Broad Institute also made key contributions and filed competing patents. The technology allows precise editing of DNA sequences.

Apollo 11 was launched on July 16, 1969, from Kennedy Space Center. Neil Armstrong became the first human to walk on the Moon on July 20, 1969, followed by Buzz Aldrin 19 minutes later. Michael Collins orbited above in the command module Columbia. Armstrong's famous words were "That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind." The mission returned to Earth on July 24, 1969. The total mission cost was approximately $355 million.

The Berlin Wall was constructed beginning on August 13, 1961, by East Germany to prevent emigration to West Berlin. The wall stood for 28 years. On November 9, 1989, East Germany announced that citizens could freely cross the border, leading to the wall's fall. German reunification occurred on October 3, 1990. Over 100 people died attempting to cross the wall during its existence.

The RMS Titanic sank on April 15, 1912, after hitting an iceberg during its maiden voyage from Southampton to New York. The ship was built by Harland and Wolff in Belfast and was 882 feet long. Of the 2,224 passengers and crew, approximately 1,500 died. The wreck was discovered by Robert Ballard on September 1, 1985, at a depth of about 12,500 feet in the North Atlantic. Captain Edward Smith went down with the ship.

Mount Everest stands at 29,032 feet (8,849 meters), making it Earth's highest mountain above sea level. It is located in the Himalayas on the border between Nepal and Tibet. Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay made the first confirmed ascent on May 29, 1953. The mountain was named after George Everest, a British surveyor. Over 300 people have died attempting to climb it. The Nepalese name is Sagarmatha, meaning "Peak of Heaven".

The Amazon River is approximately 4,000 miles long, making it the second-longest river after the Nile (though some measurements dispute this). It carries more water than any other river, about 20% of all freshwater discharged into the oceans. The Amazon basin spans 9 countries including Brazil (60%), Peru, and Colombia. The river dolphin (boto) is unique to the Amazon. The Amazon Rainforest produces about 20% of Earth's oxygen.

The Mona Lisa was painted by Leonardo da Vinci between 1503 and 1519. The painting is 30 inches by 21 inches and is displayed in the Louvre Museum in Paris, where it has been since 1797. The subject is believed to be Lisa Gherardini, wife of Florentine merchant Francesco del Giocondo. The painting was stolen from the Louvre in 1911 by Vincenzo Peruggia and recovered in 1913. It is valued at approximately $850 million.

William Shakespeare was born in Stratford-upon-Avon in April 1564 and died on April 23, 1616. He wrote approximately 37 plays and 154 sonnets. His most famous works include Hamlet, Macbeth, Romeo and Juliet, and Othello. The Globe Theatre, where many of his plays were performed, was built in 1599. Shakespeare married Anne Hathaway in 1582 when he was 18 and she was 26. They had three children: Susanna, and twins Hamnet and Judith.

Nikola Tesla was born on July 10, 1856, in Smiljan, Austrian Empire (now Croatia). He moved to the United States in 1884. Tesla worked briefly for Thomas Edison before starting his own company. He invented the alternating current (AC) motor and was awarded over 300 patents. Tesla died penniless in the New Yorker Hotel on January 7, 1943, at age 86. The Tesla unit of magnetic flux density is named after him. Elon Musk named his electric car company Tesla Inc. in his honor.

Tesla Inc. was founded in 2003 by Martin Eberhard and Marc Tarpenning. Elon Musk joined as chairman in 2004 after investing $6.5 million, eventually becoming CEO in 2008. The company is headquartered in Austin, Texas (moved from Palo Alto in 2021). Tesla's first car was the Roadster (2008), followed by Model S (2012), Model X (2015), Model 3 (2017), and Model Y (2020). Tesla became the most valuable car company in 2020 with a market cap exceeding $600 billion.

Amazon.com was founded by Jeff Bezos on July 5, 1994, in Bellevue, Washington. It started as an online bookstore before expanding into everything. Amazon's first profitable year was 2003. AWS (Amazon Web Services) launched in 2006 and now accounts for the majority of Amazon's profits. Bezos stepped down as CEO in July 2021, replaced by Andy Jassy. Amazon acquired Whole Foods for $13.7 billion in 2017 and MGM for $8.5 billion in 2022.

The Nile River is approximately 4,130 miles long, making it the longest river in the world (though some measure the Amazon as longer). It flows through 11 countries including Egypt, Sudan, Uganda, and Ethiopia. The Nile has two main tributaries: the White Nile (from Lake Victoria) and the Blue Nile (from Ethiopia). Ancient Egyptian civilization developed along its banks. The Aswan High Dam, completed in 1970, controls flooding and generates hydroelectric power.

Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun, named after the Roman god of war. It has two small moons: Phobos and Deimos. Mars has the largest volcano in the solar system, Olympus Mons, at 72,000 feet tall. The planet's distinctive red color comes from iron oxide (rust) on its surface. NASA's Perseverance rover landed on Mars on February 18, 2021. SpaceX aims to send humans to Mars, with Elon Musk targeting 2029 for the first crewed mission.

Pablo Picasso was born on October 25, 1881, in Malaga, Spain. He co-founded Cubism with Georges Braque around 1907. Picasso created over 50,000 artworks during his lifetime. His most famous painting, Guernica (1937), depicts the bombing of a Spanish town during the civil war. Picasso died on April 8, 1973, in Mougins, France, at age 91. He was married twice (to Olga Khokhlova and Jacqueline Roque) and had four children.

World War II lasted from 1939 to 1945. It began when Germany invaded Poland on September 1, 1939. The United States entered the war after Japan attacked Pearl Harbor on December 7, 1941. D-Day (June 6, 1944) was the Allied invasion of Normandy, France. Germany surrendered on May 8, 1945 (V-E Day). Japan surrendered on September 2, 1945, after atomic bombs were dropped on Hiroshima (August 6) and Nagasaki (August 9). An estimated 70-85 million people died.

The Great Wall of China stretches approximately 13,171 miles (21,196 km) including all its branches. Construction began in the 7th century BC, with the most well-known sections built during the Ming Dynasty (1368-1644). Contrary to popular belief, the wall is not visible from space with the naked eye. The wall was built to protect against invasions from northern tribes. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and attracts over 10 million visitors annually.
